criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/repeat.sh defines a function repeat that prints its first argument repeated the number of times given by its second argument, with no separator and no trailing newline beyond one final newline.
criteria.evidence: scripts/lib/repeat.sh lines 3-12: `repeat()` loops `for ((i = 0; i < count; i++)); do printf '%s' "$value"; done` (no separator) then a single `printf '\n'` — exactly one final newline.
tests/repeat.sh line 21: `assert_repeat 'ab' 3 'ababab'` compares command-substitution output to 'ababab'; orchestrator evidence shows `bash tests/repeat.sh` exited 0, so the assertion passed.
tests/repeat.sh line 22: `assert_repeat 'x' 0 ''`; with count=0 the loop body never runs so only the final newline is emitted (stripped by `$(...)`); test exited 0.

criteria.criterion: tests/repeat.sh sources the helper and asserts those two cases plus repeat 'z' 1 -> 'z'.
criteria.evidence: tests/repeat.sh line 5: `source scripts/lib/repeat.sh`; lines 21-23 assert 'ab' 3 -> 'ababab', 'x' 0 -> '', and 'z' 1 -> 'z'.

criteria.criterion: tests/repeat.sh exits 0 when all assertions pass.
criteria.evidence: tests/repeat.sh line 2 uses `set -euo pipefail` and assert_repeat returns 1 (aborting) on mismatch; when all pass the final `if` returns 0. Orchestrator evidence: `bash tests/repeat.sh exited 0`.

scope_finding: Only scripts/lib/repeat.sh (in-scope glob scripts/lib/**) and tests/repeat.sh (explicitly in-scope) were added; no out-of-scope paths (.github/**, specs/**, .orchestrator/**) touched. Orchestrator scope check: PASS.
regression_finding: None observed — both files are new additions; no existing files modified or deleted, so no existing behaviour can regress.
security_findings: None observed — no secrets, no network access, no credential access; shell code quotes expansions properly. Minor non-blocking note: `count` is used in a bash arithmetic context, which would evaluate a non-numeric second argument arithmetically, but callers in the repo pass literal integers and this is not covered by any criterion.
reasons: All five acceptance criteria are met with direct diff evidence: the repeat function concatenates its first argument count times via printf '%s' with a single trailing newline, and tests/repeat.sh sources the helper and asserts 'ab'x3, 'x'x0, and 'z'x1.
Orchestrator evidence corroborates: integrity PASS, scope PASS, and the spec test_command `bash tests/repeat.sh` exited 0.
Changed paths stay entirely within in_scope globs and no regression or security concerns block the change.
